name: Hurlburt Field
tagline: Headquarters of Air Force Special Operations Command on Florida's Emerald Coast.
_note: Air Force special operations base (Okaloosa County, FL panhandle / Emerald Coast; Central time). CONUS full template (BAH MHA FL056 Eglin AFB, which covers the Fort Walton Beach/Okaloosa area incl. Hurlburt; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). HQ Air Force Special Operations Command (AFSOC) + 1st Special Operations Wing (AC-130 gunships, CV-22, MC-130). Adjacent to Eglin AFB (already in app). Medical: 1st Special Operations Medical Group clinic. Facts verified from official sources (hurlburt.af.mil, 1 SOMDG/TRICARE, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Florida is TRICARE East. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 32547 (Fort Walton Beach).

data.Location: Fort Walton Beach area, FL (Okaloosa County, Emerald Coast)
data.Major command: Headquarters, Air Force Special Operations Command (AFSOC)
data.Aircraft: AC-130 gunships, CV-22 Osprey, and MC-130 special operations aircraft
data.Nearby: Adjacent to Eglin AFB on Florida's Emerald Coast

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Hurlburt Field is the headquarters of Air Force Special Operations Command and home to the 1st Special Operations Wing, flying AC-130 gunships, CV-22 Ospreys, and MC-130s. It's the heart of Air Force special operations aviation.
On Florida's Emerald Coast near Fort Walton Beach and adjacent to Eglin AFB, it pairs an elite mission with sugar-white Gulf beaches and an affordable-for-the-coast cost of living.

sections.title: Emerald Coast Living Near the Beaches

data.subtitle: The Emerald Coast pairs Gulf beaches with a reasonable cost of living. The main choice is which Okaloosa-area community fits your commute and schools, with Eglin's larger community right next door.
intro: The Fort Walton Beach and Destin area is a popular Gulf-Coast spot, affordable relative to many beach markets, so BAH stretches reasonably. On-base housing is available; many live off base nearby.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across Fort Walton Beach, Mary Esther, Navarre, and Niceville, with the beaches and Eglin nearby.

recommendations.advice: Fort Walton Beach and Mary Esther sit right by Hurlburt for a short commute.
Niceville (well-regarded schools) and Navarre are popular with families a short drive out.
Destin and the beach communities put you on the Gulf, with higher costs and summer crowds.
On-base housing is available; weigh it against the local market and the nearby Eglin community.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Hurlburt Field site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Hurlburt Field has privatized on-base family housing across several neighborhoods, with additional Air Force housing available at nearby Eglin AFB.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA FL056 (Eglin AFB / Fort Walton Beach, covers Hurlburt)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Okaloosa County School District serves the area, with Niceville schools especially well regarded and Santa Rosa County (Navarre) serving families to the west.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Clinic: 1st Special Operations Medical Group (Hurlburt Field Clinic)
data.Care: Outpatient primary and some specialty care on base; inpatient, emergency, and complex care via Eglin's hospital and the area network

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the Emerald Coast's popularity and repeat special-ops tours make buying worth a look for many families.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the area and pick beachside versus inland.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing or coastal insurance costs.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect a longer tour or repeat Emerald Coast assignments (common in special ops).
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The Emerald Coast market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but the Emerald Coast's desirability and repeat tours make buying worth a look for longer assignments. Factor in coastal insurance and run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, then talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability (Hurlburt and Eglin) and get the office's read on the Emerald Coast communities before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Florida vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Okaloosa or Santa Rosa County) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at the Hurlburt clinic, with Eglin's hospital for higher-level care.

data.q: What is Hurlburt Field known for?
data.a: It's the headquarters of Air Force Special Operations Command and home to the 1st Special Operations Wing, flying AC-130 gunships, CV-22 Ospreys, and MC-130s.

data.q: How is it related to Eglin?
data.a: Hurlburt is adjacent to Eglin AFB on the Emerald Coast and shares the same BAH area; many services, including Eglin's hospital, support both.
On-base housing is available; many live off base. Fort Walton Beach and Mary Esther are closest; Niceville and Navarre are popular for schools; Destin is the beach option.
Hurlburt falls in MHA FL056 (Eglin AFB/Fort Walton Beach). Use the calculator above for your grade.
